fieldDescription: How often counts refresh while paused/open trade. Lower is more responsive but can cost more CPU.
Keybind for opening the trade popup from valid market-backed dialogs, or closing it while the popup is open. Default is F8.
When enabled, adds an Open "Enhanced Trading" option to normal market interaction dialogs. The configurable trade popup hotkey remains available.
When enabled, the stock review source button can select the Sector Market. The Sector Market sells weapons and fighter LPCs currently present in live sector markets.
When enabled, the stock review source button can select the Fixer's Market. The Fixer's Market sells virtual stock for eligible weapons and fighter LPCs.
Compatibility setting for old configs. Fixer's Market now always uses a safe runtime faction catalog plus observed market stock; this flag no longer changes catalog behavior.
Multiplier applied to buy prices in the Sector Market source. Stock is limited to weapons and fighter LPCs currently present in live sector markets.
Multiplier applied to buy prices in the Fixer's Market source. Stock is virtual and intentionally expensive.
Default sufficient-stock threshold for small-slot weapons in the stock review popup.
Default sufficient-stock threshold for medium-slot weapons in the stock review popup.
Default sufficient-stock threshold for large-slot weapons in the stock review popup.
Default sufficient-stock threshold for fighter LPCs in the stock review popup.
Developer-only toggle for stock-review debug rows, stress-test ships, color controls, and future debug UI.
Default value for the master auto-trade switch on new saves. Once a save exists, the in-popup auto-rules editor controls this.

Default for new saves. When on, auto-trade does not run automatically: candidate stacks are marked in the trade screen and you press the execute hotkey to apply the trades. When off, trades run automatically as you open submarkets.
Keybind to execute pending auto-trades while a submarket is open, when confirm-before-trading is on. Default is Z.

Default for new saves. When on, auto-sells may use the black market even with your transponder on (raising smuggling suspicion). When off, black-market sells only happen while your transponder is off. Persistent in-game toggle overrides this after a save exists.

Default for new saves. When on, auto-buys (weapons, fighter LPCs, hullmods) may use the black market even with your transponder on (raising smuggling suspicion). When off, black-market buys only happen while your transponder is off.
Auto-buy any modspec the player faction has not yet learned, subject to the hullmod blacklist.
After auto-buying a hullmod, consume the modspec and add the hullmod to the player faction's known list (same as right-click consume).
Auto-buys will not spend credits below this floor. Persistent in-game value overrides this after a save exists.
